channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: UN gravely concerned by an Afghan Taliban law that has provisions on child marriage
item.description: The United Nations expressed "grave concern" on Thursday about a new law issued by Afghanistan's Taliban government on separation in marriage which includes provisions on child marriage.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations expressed "grave concern" on Thursday about a new law issued by Afghanistan's Taliban government on separation in marriage which includes provisions on child marriage.

(Image credit: Dar Yasin)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump says he's sending 5,000 more troops to Poland
item.description: President Trump's announcement stirred confusion in Europe following weeks of changing statements from his administration about reducing the American military footprint in Europe.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump's announcement stirred confusion in Europe following weeks of changing statements from his administration about reducing the American military footprint in Europe.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Rubio doubtful of diplomacy with Cuba as Trump raises new threat of military action
item.description: President Donald Trump and America's top diplomat on Thursday again raised the specter of U.S. military intervention in Cuba, a day after the administration announced criminal charges against the island's former leader, Raúl Castro.
item.content:encoded:
President Donald Trump and America's top diplomat on Thursday again raised the specter of U.S. military intervention in Cuba, a day after the administration announced criminal charges against the island's former leader, Raúl Castro.

(Image credit: Stefano Rellandini)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump's Raúl Castro indictment sends a signal to Cuba
item.description: The indictment of Raul Castro is the latest move by the Trump administration to ratchet up pressure on the Cuban government.
item.content:encoded: The indictment of Raul Castro is the latest move by the Trump administration to ratchet up pressure on the Cuban government.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas

item.title: Palestinians drop bid for a senior U.N. role after U.S. pressure
item.description: The Palestinian delegation to the United Nations has dropped its bid to secure a top U.N. job after the U.S. threatens to revoke their visas.
item.content:encoded:
The Palestinian delegation to the United Nations has dropped its bid to secure a top U.N. job after the U.S. threatens to revoke their visas.

(Image credit: Angelina Katsanis)
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: After the sirens: Lebanon's first responders swing between duty and grief
item.description: Nearly 3,000 people have been killed and nearly 1 million have been displaced the war in southern Lebanon began in March. Nearly 400 have been killed since a ceasefire began in April.
item.content:encoded:
Nearly 3,000 people have been killed and nearly 1 million have been displaced the war in southern Lebanon began in March. Nearly 400 have been killed since a ceasefire began in April.

(Image credit: Diego Ibarra Sánchez)
item.dc:creator: Diego Ibarra Sánchez

item.title: GOP races to fund immigration enforcement. And, U.S. indicts former Cuban president
item.description: Republicans are racing to pass a $72 billion immigration enforcement package. And, the U.S. has indicted former Cuban President Raúl Castro for his alleged role in the 1996 downing of two planes.
item.content:encoded:
Republicans are racing to pass a $72 billion immigration enforcement package. And, the U.S. has indicted former Cuban President Raúl Castro for his alleged role in the 1996 downing of two planes.

(Image credit: Heather Diehl)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: As floods get worse, Britain tries a new solution: beavers
item.description: About 400 years ago, beavers were hunted to extinction across Britain. Now they're being reintroduced as little climate warriors, as communities harness their dam-building skills to mitigate flooding.
item.content:encoded:
About 400 years ago, beavers were hunted to extinction across Britain. Now they're being reintroduced as little climate warriors, as communities harness their dam-building skills to mitigate flooding.

(Image credit: Dan Kitwood)
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: Netanyahu scolds Israeli security minister for videos taunting flotilla activists
item.description: Israel's national security minister triggered a backlash after releasing videos taunting detained flotilla activists who tried to breach the blockade of Gaza, telling them they should be imprisoned.
item.content:encoded:
Israel's national security minister triggered a backlash after releasing videos taunting detained flotilla activists who tried to breach the blockade of Gaza, telling them they should be imprisoned.

(Image credit: Ohad Zwigenberg)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Ebola fears surge on the ground in Congo over rapid spread of a rare type
item.description: Healthcare workers in eastern Congo said Wednesday they are underprotected and undertrained in a rapidly spreading Ebola outbreak of a rare type of the virus in one of the world's most vulnerable places.
item.content:encoded:
Healthcare workers in eastern Congo said Wednesday they are underprotected and undertrained in a rapidly spreading Ebola outbreak of a rare type of the virus in one of the world's most vulnerable places.

(Image credit: Moses Sawasawa)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
